records.ety: [OE. hiden, huden, AS. hdan; akin to Gr. key`qein, and prob. to E. house, hut, and perh. to E. hide of an animal, and to hoard. Cf. Hoard.]
records.morph: [imp. Hid (hd); p. p. Hidden (hd"d'n), Hid; p. pr. & vb. n. Hiding (hīd"ng).]

defs: To conceal, or withdraw from sight; to put out of view; to secrete.
quotes: A city that is set on an hill can not be hid.
If circumstances lead me, I will find Where truth is hid.

defs: To withhold from knowledge; to keep secret; to refrain from avowing or confessing.
quotes: Heaven from all creatures hides the book of fate.

defs: To remove from danger; to shelter.
quotes: In the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pavilion.

defs: To lie concealed; to keep one's self out of view; to be withdrawn from sight or observation.
quotes: Bred to disguise, in public 'tis you hide.

records.ety: [AS. hīd, earlier hīged; prob. orig., land enough to support a family; cf. AS. hīwan, hīgan, members of a household, and E. hind a peasant.]

defs: A measure of land, common in Domesday Book and old English charters, the quantity of which is not well ascertained, but has been differently estimated at 80, 100, and 120 acres.

records.ety: [OE. hide, hude, AS. hd; akin to D. huid, OHG. hūt, G. haut, Icel. hūð, Dan. & Sw. hud, L. cutis, Gr. ky`tos; and cf. Gr. sky`tos skin, hide, L. scutum shield, and E. sky. √13.]

defs: The skin of an animal, either raw or dressed; -- generally applied to the undressed skins of the larger domestic animals, as oxen, horses, etc.
The human skin; -- so called in contempt.
quotes: O tiger's heart, wrapped in a woman's hide!

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hided; p. pr. & vb. n. Hiding.]
